---
title: "Statistical Power: P-value Simulation Across Sample Sizes"
author: "Karen Dworsky"
date: "2025-10-13"
format: html
execute:
  cache: true
---

## Setup

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(lterdatasampler)
library(palmerpenguins)
library(rstatix)

data(and_vertebrates)
```

## Background

Filter Cutthroat trout data and run a single 5-sample t-test as a baseline.

```{r}
trout <- and_vertebrates %>%
  filter(species == "Cutthroat trout") %>%
  drop_na(weight_g)

trout_5 <- trout %>%
  group_by(section) %>%
  slice_sample(n = 5) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  t_test(weight_g ~ section, var.equal = FALSE, detailed = FALSE)

trout_5
```

## 1 & 2. Sampling function

A function that samples `n` fish per section and runs a Welch t-test.

```{r}
trout_subber <- function(n) {
  trout %>%
    group_by(section) %>%
    slice_sample(n = n) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    t_test(weight_g ~ section, var.equal = FALSE, detailed = FALSE)
}
```

## 3. Simulate across sample sizes

Run 100 iterations each for n = 5, 10, 1000, and 5000.

```{r}
sample_sizes <- rep(c(5, 10, 1000, 5000), each = 100)
trout_results <- map_dfr(sample_sizes, trout_subber)
```

## 4. P-value distributions

```{r}
trout_results %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = p)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 30, fill = "blue", color = "black") +
  facet_wrap(~ n1, labeller = label_both) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0.05, color = "red", linetype = "dashed", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(
    x = "P-value",
    y = "Count",
    title = "Distribution of P-values Across Different Sample Sizes"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
